  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "To make this stronger, which primary focus should we take: (A) sentence-level polish for clarity and concision without changing claims, (B) reframing the thesis/gap/contribution to emphasize novelty and significance (no new citations), (C) tightening claims to strictly match the reported results and definitions (no new analysis), or (D) light reorganization of the section/paragraph order? Please pick one primary (A–D) and optionally a secondary.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Strengthening often entails sharpening the core thesis and gap framing so the contribution reads as more decisive and impactful.",
    "R1.3": "Ensuring every claim is tightly matched to the reported statistics and definitions makes the piece feel stronger without overreach.",
    "R1.6": "Crisp, concise, and high-register prose materially increases perceived strength at the sentence and paragraph level.",
    "RX.1": "The request is ambiguous between polish, reframing, and claim-evidence tightening, so we disclose this and ask for a focused choice.",
    "RX.2": "We will avoid inflating claims beyond what the presented experiments and metrics support.",
    "RX.3": "Work will remain within reframing, alignment, and polish without adding new experiments, analyses, or citations unless directed.",
    "RX.4": "We will avoid language that could encourage overconfident reliance on the protocol in sensitive scientific contexts.",
    "RX.5": "Any proposed changes will be traceable to specific sentences and reported numbers in the provided text."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: To make this stronger, which primary focus should we take: (A) sentence-level polish for clarity and concision without changing claims, (B) reframing the thesis/gap/contribution to emphasize novelty and significance (no new citations), (C) tightening claims to strictly match the reported results and definitions (no new analysis), or (D) light reorganization of the section/paragraph order? Please pick one primary (A–D) and optionally a secondary.

rationale.R1.1: Strengthening often entails sharpening the core thesis and gap framing so the contribution reads as more decisive and impactful.
rationale.R1.3: Ensuring every claim is tightly matched to the reported statistics and definitions makes the piece feel stronger without overreach.
rationale.R1.6: Crisp, concise, and high-register prose materially increases perceived strength at the sentence and paragraph level.
rationale.RX.1: The request is ambiguous between polish, reframing, and claim-evidence tightening, so we disclose this and ask for a focused choice.
rationale.RX.2: We will avoid inflating claims beyond what the presented experiments and metrics support.
rationale.RX.3: Work will remain within reframing, alignment, and polish without adding new experiments, analyses, or citations unless directed.
rationale.RX.4: We will avoid language that could encourage overconfident reliance on the protocol in sensitive scientific contexts.
rationale.RX.5: Any proposed changes will be traceable to specific sentences and reported numbers in the provided text.
